mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and rising input costs, which compress corporate margins and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the mechanism shows no regime-dependent alpha across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a lagging federal funds rate creates real negative interest rates, prompting equity market repricing and negative forward returns as investors anticipate aggressive central bank tightening.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL exceeds 320 while FEDFUNDS remains below 4.0
failure_condition: The joint condition of high CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS yields positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of periods, or Rank IC drops below 0.015 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction signals economic recession, immediately triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth falls below zero
failure_condition: Negative GDPC1 growth is followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the signal shows no predictive accuracy with Rank IC below 0.02 in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling industrial production indicates a supply-demand mismatch and weakening economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and falling INDPRO produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term loses predictive power in walk-forward regression tests.
